ground_truth.title: We asked four AI coding agents to rebuild Minesweeper—the results were explosive
ground_truth.author: Kyle Orland and Benj Edwards
ground_truth.main_content: The idea of using AI to help with computer programming has become a contentious issue. To see how effective these modern AI coding tools are becoming, we decided to test four major models with a simple task: re-creating the classic Windows game Minesweeper. The prompt asked for a full-featured web version with sound effects that replicates the standard Windows game and implements a surprise, fun gameplay feature, including mobile touchscreen support. We tested four AI coding agents: OpenAI's Codex based on GPT-5, Anthropic's Claude Code with Opus 4.5, Google's Gemini CLI, and Mistral Vibe. Ars Senior Gaming Editor Kyle Orland judged each example blind, without knowing which model generated which Minesweeper clone. For this test, we used each AI model unmodified code in a single-shot result to see how well these tools perform without any human debugging. Mistral Vibe got many basics right but left out chording and didn't perform well on presentational touches - rating 4/10. OpenAI Codex impressed with chording implementation and cute presentation touches, rating 9/10. Anthropic Claude Code had strong presentation and Power Mode options but lacked chording, rating 7/10. Google Gemini CLI completely failed, producing only gray boxes with no playable game.
with: The idea of using AI to help with computer programming has become a contentious issue.
To see how effective these modern AI coding tools are becoming, we decided to test four major models with a simple task: re-creating the classic Windows game Minesweeper.
Ars Senior Gaming Editor (and Minesweeper expert) Kyle Orland then judged each example blind, without knowing which model generated which Minesweeper clone.
Not only did this agent include the crucial chording feature, but it also included on-screen instructions for using it on both PC and mobile browsers.
Gemini CLI did give us a few gray boxes you can click, but the playfields are missing.

items.caption: Which mines are mine, and which are AI? Credit: Aurich Lawson | Getty Images
Just ignore that Custom button. It's purely for show. Credit: Benj Edwards
I can't tell you how much I appreciate those chording instructions at the bottom. Credit: Benj Edwards
The Power Mod powers on display here make even Expert boards pretty trivial to complete. Credit: Benj Edwards
So... where's the game? Credit: Benj Edwards
